question: What type of operating system is Android?
description: Android is an open-source mobile OS developed by Google.

question: In which year was the first Android device launched?
description: The first commercial Android device (HTC Dream / T-Mobile G1) launched in 2008.

question: Which kernel is Android based on?
description: Android is built on the Linux kernel.

question: Which programming languages are primarily used to write Android applications?
description: Android apps are mainly written in Java and Kotlin.

question: Which markup language is used to design the UI/UX of Android apps?
description: Android layouts are written in XML.

question: Which Google product is a specialized Android interface designed for televisions?
description: Android TV is the TV-optimised variant. Wear OS targets watches; Android Auto targets cars.

question: What does an API Level uniquely identify?
option_b: The framework API revision offered by a version of the Android platform
option_d: The screen resolution of the device
description: An API Level is an integer identifying the framework API revision of an Android version.

question: How many main layers/components does the Android OS architecture have?
description: Android architecture has 5 layers: Linux Kernel, Native Libraries, Android Runtime, Application Framework, and Applications.

question: Which component is considered the 'heart' of the Android architecture?
description: The Linux kernel is the foundation ('heart') of the architecture.

question: Which layer of Android architecture is responsible for device drivers, power management, and memory management?
description: The Linux kernel handles drivers, power, and memory management.

question: Which Native Library is responsible for browser support in Android?
description: WebKit powers browser/web rendering. SQLite = database, FreeType = fonts, SSL = security.

question: What is the purpose of SQLite in Android's Native Libraries?
description: SQLite is a lightweight embedded database for local data storage.

question: Which Native Library handles playing and recording audio and video formats?
description: The Media framework handles audio/video playback and recording.

question: What does DVM stand for in Android Runtime?
description: DVM = Dalvik Virtual Machine, the runtime that executed Android apps (now largely replaced by ART).

question: How does the Dalvik Virtual Machine (DVM) differ from the Java Virtual Machine (JVM)?
option_c: DVM is optimized for mobile, consumes less memory, and provides fast performance
description: The DVM is tuned for resource-constrained mobile devices, using less memory than a standard JVM.

question: Which Application Framework component controls the application lifecycle and activity stack?
description: The Activity Manager manages the lifecycle and back-stack of activities.

question: Which Application Framework component allows apps to publish and share data with other applications?
description: Content Providers expose and share data between apps.

question: Which Application Framework component provides access to non-code embedded resources such as strings and color settings?
description: The Resource Manager provides access to strings, colours, and layouts under res/.

question: At which layer are end-user apps such as Contacts, Browser, and Games installed?
description: User-facing apps live in the top Applications layer.

question: Which folder in the Android project structure contains AndroidManifest.xml?
description: AndroidManifest.xml lives in the manifests folder.

question: What does the 'java' folder in an Android project contain?
option_c: Java source code files for the app
description: The java folder holds the app's Java/Kotlin source code.

question: Which subfolder of 'res' stores images used by the app?
description: res/drawable stores general images and drawables.

question: Which subfolder of 'res' contains the launcher icons for the app?
description: Launcher icons go in res/mipmap so they scale correctly across densities.

question: Which subfolder of 'res' contains XML files describing menus in the app?
description: Menu definitions live in res/menu.

question: Where are resources such as strings and colors stored in an Android project?
description: Strings, colours, dimens, and styles are stored in res/values.

question: To run an app on an emulator, which step must be done first in Android Studio?
option_b: Select Run > Run 'app' or click the Run icon in the toolbar
description: Click the Run icon (or Run > Run 'app') to build and launch on the emulator.

question: What must be enabled on an Android device before running an app from Android Studio via USB?
description: USB Debugging (in Developer options) must be enabled to deploy over USB.

question: How do you access Developer options on an Android device?
option_b: Tap Build number seven times in Settings > About phone
option_c: Connect to Wi-Fi and go to Settings
description: Tapping Build number seven times unlocks the hidden Developer options menu.

question: What hardware is needed to run an app on a physical Android device from Android Studio?
option_b: A data cable to connect via USB port
description: A USB data cable connects the device to the development machine.

question: On Windows, what may be required before running an app on a physical device?
description: Windows often needs the device's USB driver installed to recognise it.

question: After selecting your physical device in Android Studio and clicking OK, what does it do?
option_a: Publishes the app to the Play Store
option_b: Installs the app on the device and runs it
option_c: Creates a backup of the device
description: Android Studio installs and launches the app on the selected device.

question: What is the recommended practice regarding the emulator during a development session?
option_c: Start the emulator once and keep it open until done testing
option_d: Only use a real device, never an emulator
description: Emulators are slow to boot, so keep one running throughout the session.

question: In Android Studio, where can you find the list of available emulators and connected devices?
description: The device chooser is reached via Run > Select Device (or the toolbar device dropdown).

question: How many main application components can be used within an Android application?
description: The 4 core components are Activities, Services, Broadcast Receivers, and Content Providers.

question: Which Android component dictates the UI and handles user interaction with the screen?
description: An Activity represents a single screen and handles user interaction.

question: Which component handles background processing associated with an application?
description: Services run long-running operations in the background, without a UI.

question: Which component handles communication between the Android OS and applications?
description: Broadcast Receivers respond to system-wide broadcast messages (e.g. battery low, boot completed).

question: Which component handles data and database management issues in Android?
description: Content Providers manage shared app data and database access.

question: Which additional component represents a behavior or a portion of user interface within an Activity?
description: A Fragment is a reusable portion of UI/behaviour hosted inside an Activity.

question: What are Views in Android application components?
option_b: View hierarchies that control screen format
option_c: UI elements drawn on screen including images, texts, and buttons
option_d: External elements such as strings and constants
description: Views are the individual on-screen UI widgets (TextView, Button, ImageView, etc.).

question: What are Layouts in Android application components?
option_b: View hierarchies that control screen format and the appearance of Views
description: A Layout is a container/view hierarchy that arranges Views on the screen.

question: Which component serves as the configuration file for an Android application?
description: AndroidManifest.xml is the app's configuration file, declaring components and permissions.

question: Which Android component stores external elements such as strings, constants, and drawable pictures?
description: Resources hold external assets (strings, colours, drawables) under res/.

question: Is Android a closed-source operating system?
option_c: Yes — but only the kernel is open
description: Android is open-source (the Android Open Source Project).

question: Can Android applications be written only in Java?
option_a: Yes — Java is the only option
option_b: No — Kotlin (and others) can also be used
description: Both Java and Kotlin are first-class Android languages (plus C++ via NDK, Dart via Flutter, etc.).

question: Is the Linux kernel the topmost layer of the Android architecture?
option_a: Yes — it sits at the top
option_b: No — it is the bottom layer; Applications is the top
description: The Linux kernel is the bottom layer; the Applications layer is on top.

question: Is WebKit (in Android's Native Libraries) responsible for database management?
option_b: No — WebKit handles browser/web rendering; SQLite handles databases
description: WebKit = browser engine; SQLite = database management.

question: Does the 'drawable' subfolder of 'res' store the launcher icons for the app?
option_a: Yes — launcher icons live in drawable
option_b: No — launcher icons are stored in mipmap; drawable holds general images
option_d: No — they are stored in values
description: Launcher icons belong in res/mipmap; res/drawable holds general images.

question: What does AndroidManifest.xml describe?
option_b: All the components of an Android app (and permissions)
description: The manifest declares all components (activities, services, receivers, providers) plus permissions and app metadata.

question: Does the 'Services' component dictate the UI and handle user interaction?
option_b: No — Activities manage the UI; Services run background work
description: Activities handle the UI; Services handle background processing.

question: Which build system does Android Studio use?
description: Android Studio uses Gradle as its build system.
